edges.description: Impaired triacylglycerol hydrolysis causes lipid-droplet accumulation in muscle.

nodes.description: Undegraded triacylglycerol accumulates as cytoplasmic lipid droplets in skeletal and cardiac muscle, driving progressive myopathy and cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Triglyceride deposit cardiomyovasculopathy: a rare cardiovascular disorder.
evidence.snippet:
PNPLA2 encoding adipose
triglyceride lipase (ATGL) which releases long chain fatty acid (LCFA) as a
major energy source by the intracellular TG hydrolysis

nodes.description: Biallelic PNPLA2 variants reduce adipose triglyceride lipase (ATGL), the rate-limiting enzyme for intracellular triacylglycerol hydrolysis.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral Lipid Storage Diseases: clinical/genetic features and natural history in a large cohort of Italian patients.
evidence.snippet: Neutral Lipid Storage Diseases (NLSDs: NLSD type M with Myopathy and NLSD type I with Ichthyosis)
